_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-national autopilot)
_meta.note: Federal House batch: FL-09 Soto / FL-10 Frost / FL-11 Webster / FL-12 Bilirakis / FL-14 Castor. Status web-verified: Soto, Frost, Castor, Bilirakis all running for re-election 2026 (Ballotpedia, Aug 18 2026 primaries); Webster RETIRING — announced 2026-04-28 he will not seek re-election (Roll Call / Florida Politics), seat open 2026. Key catches: Soto voted AYE to ban noncitizen voting in DC (Roll 232, 2024) and is aggressively pro-crypto (FIT21/CLARITY/GENIUS yeas, Blockchain Caucus co-chair) while voting twice to keep a Fed CBDC path open; Frost (Heritage 26% in 118th, oddly high) and Castor both voted YEA on H.Res 894 declaring anti-Zionism antisemitism while 92 Dems voted present; Frost voted NAY on the Israel supplemental; Webster and Bilirakis both voted NAY on the April 2024 Ukraine supplemental; Webster took $49,550 and Bilirakis $19,250 in AIPAC PAC money (OpenSecrets 2023-24). Scored from House Clerk roll-call XML, congress.gov cosponsor lists, Heritage Action, SBA Pro-Life, NRA/Giffords, OpenSecrets.

profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 from Clerk roll calls (117th-119th), Heritage Action (5% lifetime), Giffords/SBA, OpenSecrets. 2026 re-election bid web-verified (Ballotpedia, Dem primary 8/18/2026). Religion not reliably documented — left blank.

darren-soto.notes_set: FL-09 (Kissimmee/Orlando) since 2017; first Floridian of Puerto Rican descent in Congress; Energy & Commerce. Heritage Action 5% lifetime. Progressive on life (WHPA yea, Born-Alive nay, Planned Parenthood endorsed 2024), marriage/gender (RFMA + Equality Act yeas), elections (HR 1 yea, SAVE nay twice), border (HR 2 + Laken Riley nays, Dream Act original cosponsor), guns (AWB 2022 yea + cosponsor, BSCA yea, Giffords endorsed). DEVIATIONS: voted AYE to prohibit noncitizen voting in DC elections (HR 192, Roll 232, 5/2024); co-chair Congressional Blockchain Caucus — yea on FIT21, CLARITY and GENIUS crypto bills with GOP majorities, though he voted NAY twice on the Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act. Voted yea on all April 2024 foreign-aid supplementals (Israel, Ukraine, Indo-Pacific). NRCC attacked his Laken Riley nay (2/2026).

0.note: Voted YEA on Women's Health Protection Act (HR 3755, Roll 295, 2021) and NAY on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (HR 21, Roll 27, 1/2025).

1.note: Voted to codify nationwide abortion access (WHPA); campaign runs on 'Reproductive Rights' plank.

4.note: Planned Parenthood Action 100% rating and 2024 PP endorsement documented, but direct abortion-industry PAC dollars not verified this pass — null on the funding question.

0.note: Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act final passage (Roll 513, 12/2022); own release: 'Soto Votes to Enshrine Marriage Equality.'

1.note: RFMA yea + Equality Act yea (HR 5, Roll 39, 2021).

2.note: Equality Act yea (enshrines gender identity in federal civil-rights law); hosted Transgender Day of Visibility roundtable opposing 'anti-LGBTQI+ legislation.'

4.note: Equality Act yea — federal promotion of LGBTQ+ identity across public policy.

1.note: Voted NO on Parents Bill of Rights Act (HR 5, Roll 161, 3/2023), which included parental-notification provisions for school gender-identity changes.

4.note: Equality Act yea — would bar faith-based agencies from declining same-sex placements (no religious exemption; limits RFRA defenses).

1.note: Equality Act yea — the bill expressly strips RFRA conscience defenses for medical professionals, business owners and agencies.

0.note: Voted NAY twice on the Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act (HR 5403 Roll 230, 2024; HR 1919 Roll 201, 2025) — kept a Fed CBDC path open. Mixed: strongly pro-crypto (FIT21/CLARITY/GENIUS yeas, Blockchain Caucus co-chair), but the CBDC half of this question is a documented contrary vote.

1.note: Voted NO on the SAVE Act (citizenship verification for voter registration) in both 2024 (Roll 345) and 2025 (Roll 102). Deviation noted: AYE on HR 192 banning noncitizen voting in DC (Roll 232, 2024).

2.note: Voted YEA on HR 1 For the People Act (Roll 62, 2021) — nationwide early/mail voting expansion, the opposite of single-day in-person voting.

3.note: HR 1 yea — mandates no-excuse mail voting and drop boxes; touted the bill in his own release.

0.note: Voted NAY on HR 2 Secure the Border Act (Roll 209, 5/2023) — wall construction and border enforcement package.

1.note: Voted NAY on Laken Riley Act final passage (S 5, Roll 23, 1/2025), citing mass-deportation concerns; original cosponsor of the Dream and Promise Act and public DACA defender.

3.note: HR 2 nay — the bill mandated nationwide E-Verify (marquee party-line border vote).

1.note: Voted YEA on (and cosponsored) the Assault Weapons Ban of 2022 (HR 1808, Roll 410); yea on the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act red-flag funding (Roll 299); Giffords endorsed.

4.note: AWB cosponsorship/yea is a documented citizen-disarmament initiative.

3.note: AIPAC/foreign-lobby receipts not confirmed for Soto this pass — null.

0.note: Voted NO on the Freedom for Health Care Workers Act (repealing the CMS health-worker vaccine mandate, Roll 98, 2023) and NAY on terminating the COVID national emergency (HJ Res 7, Roll 104, 2023).

profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 from Clerk roll calls (118th-119th), Heritage Action (26%, 118th), Giffords, congress.gov. 2026 re-election bid web-verified (Ballotpedia, Dem primary 8/18/2026); not seeking another office. Self-identifies as Christian; has publicly condemned 'Christian Nationalism.'

maxwell-frost.notes_set: FL-10 (Orlando) since 2023; youngest House member when elected (b. 1997, Afro-Cuban); former March for Our Lives national organizing director — gun control is his signature issue (platform: assault-weapons ban, magazine limits, 'dismantle the NRA'; Giffords endorsed; Everytown-endorsed gun-violence research bill). Progressive on life (Born-Alive nay 2023 + 2025, NARAL 100%), gender (HR 734 + HR 28 women's-sports nays, Equality Act cosponsor), elections (SAVE nay twice, also NO on banning noncitizen voting in DC), border (HR 2 + Laken Riley nays). Took office after the RFMA vote (12/2022) — no marriage roll call. DEVIATIONS: YEA on H.Res 894 declaring anti-Zionism antisemitism while 92 Dems voted present (Roll 697, 12/2023); Heritage Action 26% in the 118th, unusually high for a progressive; AYE on the 2023 debt-limit deal. Voted NAY on the April 2024 Israel supplemental (yea on Ukraine); listed by Track AIPAC as rejecting AIPAC support (unverified).

0.note: Voted NAY on the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act twice (HR 26 Roll 29, 2023; HR 21 Roll 27, 2025).

1.note: Reproductive Freedom for All (NARAL) 100% score; floor advocacy for abortion access; demanded investigation of crisis-pregnancy-center funding.
Cosponsor of the Equality Act (HR 15, 118th). Took office Jan 2023 — no RFMA roll call.

2.note: Voted NAY on the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act twice (HR 734 Roll 192, 2023; HR 28 Roll 12, 2025); floor remarks against trans sports bans.

4.note: Equality Act cosponsorship + recorded votes against both women's-sports bills.

1.note: Voted NO on the Parents Bill of Rights Act (HR 5, Roll 161, 3/2023).

4.note: Equality Act cosponsor — would bar faith-based agencies from declining same-sex placements.

1.note: Equality Act cosponsor (strips RFRA conscience defenses); voted NO on repealing the CMS health-worker vaccine mandate (HR 497, Roll 98).

0.note: Voted NO on the CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (HR 5403, Roll 230, 5/2024).

1.note: Voted NO on the SAVE Act twice (Rolls 345/2024, 102/2025) and NO on HR 192 barring noncitizen voting in DC elections (Roll 232, 2024).

0.note: Voted NAY on HR 2 Secure the Border Act (Roll 209, 5/2023).

1.note: Voted NAY on the Laken Riley Act both times (HR 29 Roll 6 and S 5 final Roll 23, 1/2025).

3.note: HR 2 nay — the bill mandated nationwide E-Verify (marquee party-line border vote).

1.note: Career gun-control organizer (March for Our Lives national organizing director); platform: assault-weapons ban, high-capacity-magazine limits, universal background checks; Giffords endorsed.

4.note: Platform pledge to 'dismantle the NRA'; introduced Everytown-endorsed National Gun Violence Research Act.

3.note: Listed by Track AIPAC as rejecting AIPAC support — activist-sourced, not verified against FEC this pass; null.

0.note: Voted NO on the Freedom for Health Care Workers Act (Roll 98, 2023) and NAY on terminating the COVID national emergency (HJ Res 7, Roll 104, 2023).

profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 from Clerk roll calls, congress.gov cosponsor lists, own press releases, Heritage Action, SBA, OpenSecrets. RETIREMENT web-verified: announced 2026-04-28 he will not seek re-election (Roll Call, Florida Politics, Ballotpedia) — will finish his eighth term; FL-11 seat open 2026.

daniel-webster.notes_set: FL-11 (The Villages) since 2011; former FL House Speaker (1996-98, first GOP speaker since Reconstruction). Southern Baptist (First Baptist Church of Central Florida). RETIRING: announced 2026-04-28 — 'time has come to pass the torch'; seat open 2026. Strong social-conservative record: Life at Conception Act cosponsor, Born-Alive reintroducer, SBA A+; NO on Respect for Marriage Act and Equality Act (own opposition statement); original cosponsor + yes on the women's-sports bill; authored the My Child, My Choice Act (parental consent on SOGI instruction). SAVE Act yes (2024) + SAVE America Act yes (2026); HR 2 + Laken Riley yes. Maverick streak: ran for Speaker against leadership three times (2015 vs Boehner, Oct 2015 with Freedom Caucus backing, 2017); NO on the Dec 2022 omnibus; NO on the April 2024 Ukraine supplemental ('blank check') — but AYE on the 2023 debt-limit deal. Voted to pass the Balanced Budget Amendment (HJRes 139, 3/2026). NRA A. AIPAC gave $49,550 in the 2023-24 cycle (OpenSecrets). Heritage 94% (117th), lifetime ~78%.

0.note: Cosponsor of the Life at Conception Act (HR 431, 118th) — personhood from conception.

1.note: Life at Conception Act cosponsorship is abolition-tier (full legal personhood, not merely restrictions); also reintroduced the Born-Alive Act.

4.note: SBA Pro-Life A+; no abortion-industry PAC money found on OpenSecrets.

0.note: Voted NO on the Respect for Marriage Act final passage (Roll 513, 12/2022).

1.note: RFMA no + Equality Act no with own opposition statement ('HR 5 Hurts Families, Women & Vulnerable Children').

2.note: Original cosponsor and yes vote on the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (HR 734, 2023).

4.note: Equality Act opposition statement + authored the My Child, My Choice Act against SOGI instruction without parental consent.

1.note: Yes on the Parents Bill of Rights Act (Roll 161, 2023 — only 5 GOP defections; his support documented in his own parents'-rights resolution).

2.note: Authored the My Child, My Choice Act — parental consent required before sexual-orientation/gender-identity instruction in schools.

1.note: Opposed the Equality Act (vote + statement), which would strip RFRA conscience defenses for medical professionals, businesses and agencies.

0.note: Individual Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act vote not verified this pass — null pending roll-call check.

2.note: Voted to pass the Balanced Budget Amendment (HJRes 139, 3/2026); voted against the Dec 2022 omnibus. Caveat: AYE on the 2023 debt-limit deal (Roll 243).

1.note: Yes on the SAVE Act (7/2024) and the SAVE America Act (2/2026) — proof of citizenship + photo ID.

0.note: Yes on HR 2 Secure the Border Act (Roll 209, 2023); cosponsored two component bills.

1.note: Laken Riley cosponsor + yes (mandatory detention of criminal aliens) — but no documented position on mandatory deportation of ALL illegal aliens including those who entered as minors; null.

3.note: HR 2 yes — the bill mandated nationwide E-Verify.

1.note: NRA A rating (92% in 2022 per VoteSmart); not among the 14 GOP yeas on the 2022 Bipartisan Safer Communities red-flag bill.

4.note: NRA A rating — single-issue grade covering opposition to disarmament initiatives.

1.note: NO on the April 2024 Ukraine supplemental ('can't vote for a blank check') — a restraint signal, but no AUMF-repeal or withdrawal vote documented; null.

3.note: AIPAC gave $49,550 in the 2023-24 cycle per OpenSecrets contributor data.

0.note: Co-introduced the CRA resolution to nullify the Biden OSHA vaccine mandate (11/2021, with Rep. Keller and 160 colleagues).

profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 from Clerk roll-call XML (verified individual votes), congress.gov cosponsor lists, Heritage Action (77% lifetime), SBA (A+), OpenSecrets. 2026 re-election bid (11th term) web-verified (Ballotpedia, Florida Politics; GOP primary 8/18/2026).

gus-bilirakis.notes_set: FL-12 (Palm Harbor/Pasco) since 2007, succeeded father Michael Bilirakis; Greek Orthodox; co-chair International Religious Freedom Caucus and Hellenic Caucus — active advocate for persecuted Christians (Copts in Egypt, Ecumenical Patriarchate in Turkey). Solid social-conservative roll-call record: Life at Conception Act cosponsor (118th + 119th), SBA A+, NAY on RFMA both times, NAY Equality Act, YEA women's-sports bill, AYE Parents Bill of Rights, SAVE Act yes twice, HR 2 + Laken Riley yes, NAY on both the 2022 gun bill (own release attacked its red-flag provisions) and the assault-weapons ban; AYE Anti-CBDC Act. More leadership-aligned on spending: Heritage lifetime 77% (100% only in the 117th); AYE on the 2023 debt-limit deal — though NAY on the FY23 omnibus. DEVIATION: NAY on the April 2024 Ukraine supplemental final passage. AIPAC was his #2 donor ($19,250, 2023-24, OpenSecrets); top industry: health professionals.

0.note: Life at Conception Act cosponsor in the 118th (HR 431) and 119th (HR 722) — personhood from conception.

1.note: Life at Conception Act cosponsorship is abolition-tier; also yea on the Born-Alive Act (Roll 29, 2023).

4.note: SBA Pro-Life A+ (2023); no abortion-industry PAC money on OpenSecrets (top donors: health professionals, AIPAC).

0.note: Voted NAY on the Respect for Marriage Act both times (Rolls 373 and 513, 2022) — not among the GOP yes votes.

1.note: RFMA nay twice + Equality Act nay (Roll 39, 2021).

2.note: Yea on the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (HR 734, Roll 192, 2023).

1.note: AYE on the Parents Bill of Rights Act (Roll 161, 3/2023).

0.note: Signature INTERNATIONAL religious-freedom advocacy (IRF Caucus co-chair; Copts, Ecumenical Patriarchate) — but no documented position on domestic public-square profession; null with credit noted.

1.note: Cosponsored + voted AYE on repealing the CMS health-worker vaccine mandate (HR 497, Roll 98 — medical conscience); Equality Act nay (preserves RFRA conscience defenses).

0.note: AYE on the CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (HR 5403, Roll 230, 5/2024).

2.note: Mixed record: NAY on the FY23 $1.7T omnibus (Roll 549) but AYE on the 2023 debt-limit deal (Roll 243); no BBA vote found; Heritage lifetime 77%. Null.

1.note: AYE on the SAVE Act twice (Roll 345, 2024; Roll 102, 2025) — citizenship verification for voter registration.

0.note: YEA on HR 2 Secure the Border Act (Roll 209, 5/2023).

1.note: Laken Riley yea twice (mandatory detention of criminal aliens) — but no documented position on mandatory deportation of ALL illegal aliens; null.

3.note: HR 2 yea — the bill mandated nationwide E-Verify.

1.note: NAY on the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act with own release attacking its red-flag/due-process provisions (Roll 299, 2022); NAY on the Assault Weapons Ban (Roll 410, 2022).

4.note: NRA A rating / NRA-PVF endorsement; voted against the 2022 disarmament bills.

3.note: AIPAC was his #2 cycle donor ($19,250, 2023-24) per OpenSecrets.

0.note: Cosponsored and voted AYE on the Freedom for Health Care Workers Act repealing the CMS COVID vaccine mandate (Roll 98, 2023).

profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 from Clerk roll-call XML (verified individual votes), Heritage Action (0% in 119th), Giffords, SBA, her own releases. 2026 re-election bid web-verified (Ballotpedia: Dem primary 8/18/2026 vs Juan Arauz; competitive post-redistricting general).

kathy-castor.notes_set: FL-14 (Tampa) since 2007; daughter of FL politician Betty Castor; Presbyterian; chaired the House Select Committee on the Climate Crisis (2019-23, authored its Climate Crisis Action Plan; introduced the Climate Action Now Act, HR 9). Heritage Action 0% (119th) / 5% (118th). Progressive across the board on documented roll calls: WHPA yea, Born-Alive nay, RFMA + Equality Act yeas, women's-sports nay (defended 'safe spaces' for transgender students), Parents Bill of Rights no, HR 1 yea, SAVE Act no twice, HR 2 + Laken Riley nays (not among the Dem crossovers), assault-weapons-ban yea, Giffords endorsed, anti-CBDC-ban no. DEVIATIONS: YEA on H.Res 894 declaring anti-Zionism antisemitism while 92 Dems voted present (Roll 697, 12/2023); yea on the April 2024 Israel and Ukraine supplementals with a strong pro-Israel statement after Oct 7. AIPAC receipts unverified. Industry-capture cells left null — no COVID-mandate or pharma-liability votes located this pass.

0.note: YEA on the Women's Health Protection Act (Roll 295, 2021); NAY on the Born-Alive Act (Roll 29, 2023); SBA scorecard: consistently voted against protections for the unborn.

1.note: Voted to codify nationwide abortion access (WHPA).

4.note: Pro-PP scorecard record documented, but EMILY's List / abortion-industry PAC dollar amounts not verified this pass — null on the funding question.

1.note: RFMA yea + Equality Act yea (Roll 39, 2021).

4.note: Equality Act yea — federal promotion of LGBTQ+ identity across public policy.

4.note: Equality Act yea — would bar faith-based agencies from declining same-sex placements.

1.note: Equality Act yea — the bill expressly strips RFRA conscience defenses.

1.note: Voted NO on the SAVE Act twice (Roll 345, 2024; Roll 102, 2025).

2.note: YEA on HR 1 For the People Act (Roll 62, 2021) — nationwide early/mail voting expansion.

3.note: HR 1 yea — mandates no-excuse mail voting and drop boxes.

0.note: NAY on HR 2 Secure the Border Act (Roll 209, 5/2023).

4.note: AWB yea — a documented citizen-disarmament initiative.
